title: Chrysanthemum Tea

seo.title: How to Make Chrysanthemum Tea — Easy Chinese Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Chrysanthemum Tea. This easy Chinese recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Chrysanthemum Tea — a beloved Chinese classic

description: Chrysanthemum Tea is a fragrant and refreshing herbal infusion cherished in Chinese culture for its delicate floral aroma and cooling properties. Made from dried chrysanthemum flowers, this soothing beverage is often enjoyed hot or cold, offering a naturally sweet and mildly bitter taste that is both calming and invigorating. It is widely believed to promote well-being and is a popular choice in dim sum restaurants and homes alike.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chrysanthemum Flowers
instructions.text: Measure out the dried chrysanthemum flowers and place them in a heatproof teapot or a large mug. Ensure your teapot or mug is clean and ready for steeping.

instructions.title: Rinse the Flowers Briefly
instructions.text: Pour a small amount of hot water (not boiling) over the chrysanthemum flowers, swirl gently for a few seconds, then quickly discard this water. This initial rinse helps to wash away any dust and prepares the flowers for optimal steeping.

instructions.title: Boil Fresh Water
instructions.text: Bring fresh, filtered water to a rolling boil in a kettle or saucepan. The quality of water greatly impacts the taste of your tea, so filtered water is highly recommended for the best flavor.

instructions.title: Steep the Tea
instructions.text: Carefully pour the freshly boiled water over the rinsed chrysanthemum flowers. Cover the teapot or mug with a lid to trap the heat and allow the flowers to steep for 3 to 5 minutes. Steeping longer will result in a stronger, potentially more bitter tea.

instructions.title: Sweeten to Taste (Optional)
instructions.text: If desired, add rock sugar or honey to the tea while it is still hot, stirring until completely dissolved. Adjust the sweetness according to your personal preference. For a truly authentic experience, use small pieces of rock sugar.

instructions.title: Serve and Enjoy
instructions.text: Strain the tea into individual cups, or serve directly from the teapot, allowing the beautiful flowers to remain for visual appeal. Enjoy your Chrysanthemum Tea hot for a warming beverage, or let it cool to room temperature before chilling it in the refrigerator for a refreshing cold drink.

tips: For the best flavor, always use high-quality, food-grade dried chrysanthemum flowers. Look for ones that are vibrant in color and have a fresh aroma.
Adjust the steeping time based on your preference for strength. A shorter steep yields a lighter, more delicate tea, while a longer steep produces a richer flavor.
Chrysanthemum tea is excellent served both hot and cold. For a cold version, steep as usual, let it cool, then chill in the refrigerator and serve over ice with a slice of lemon or a sprig of mint.
article: Chrysanthemum Tea is a beloved Chinese Beverages dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
